channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: High Court orders inquiry after lawyers allege assault in Lucknow court
item.description: The Allahabad High Court has directed the Lucknow police commissioner and district judge to file detailed reports following allegations that three Delhi-based lawyers and their client were assaulted w...

item.title: US and Lebanon leaders hold bilateral meeting in Washington
item.description: US President Donald Trump and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun convened a bilateral meeting in the Oval Office on July 21, 2026, to discuss regional tensions. The meeting followed reports of heightened ...

item.title: Belgian Cyclist Remco Evenepoel Triumphs in Tour de France Individual Time-Trial
item.description: Belgian cyclist Remco Evenepoel secured a dominant victory in the 16th stage of the 113th Tour de France, a 26.1km individual time-trial held on Belgium's national holiday. The 26-year-old world and O...

item.title: Mexican tourist killed by tractor trailer during ICE encounter in Florida
item.description: A Mexican man, Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán, died after being struck by a tractor trailer while fleeing from immigration officers in Florida. The incident occurred on July 14 when he darted across a bus...

item.title: Senate Senator Condemns Defense Secretary's Remarks on Iran War Funding
item.description: During a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing, U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth faced sharp criticism from Democratic Senator Jeanne Shaheen over the administration's handling of the Iran war...

item.title: GOP Lawmaker Warns McConnell on Senate Majority and Health Crisis
item.description: A Republican lawmaker has issued an ultimatum to Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell, warning that his prolonged absence from Congress could jeopardize the GOP's narrow Senate majority. Senator Tom...

item.title: Sikh Man Removed from Gym for Wearing Kirpan, Legal Rights at Stake
item.description: A Sikh man was asked to leave a GoodLife gym after being identified with a kirpan, sparking debate over religious rights and workplace policies. The incident highlights tensions between religious prac...

item.title: Burnham outlines cost of living priorities and funding challenges in first cabinet meeting
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham has prioritised addressing living costs during his first cabinet meeting, announcing a VAT cut on household energy bills from October. The policy, aimed at saving typical h...

item.title: BC Schools to Continue SOGI 123 Program Amid Political Claims
item.description: The SOGI 123 program, which aims to create inclusive school environments for students of all sexual orientations and gender identities, will remain in place in British Columbia schools despite claims ...

item.description: Planning Minister Chris Steel has unveiled a draft vision for the southern gateway corridor between Parliament House and Woden Valley, aiming to increase mid-rise housing to better connect Canberra's ...

item.title: High Court Criticizes Corruption Amid Ram Temple Donation Theft
item.description: The Allahabad High Court's Justice Atul Sreedharan has delivered a scathing critique of systemic corruption in Indian institutions, using the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayo...

item.title: Evenepoel narrows Pogacar's lead in Tour de France time trial
item.description: Belgian cyclist Remco Evenepoel secured victory in the individual time trial on stage 16 of the Tour de France, reducing Slovenian champion Tadej Pogacar's overall lead from four minutes and 32 second...

item.title: PFIPC Unrecognized by FG: Ministry Clarifies No Official Ties
item.description: The Federal Ministry of Foreign Affairs has denied any recognition or engagement with the controversial Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), revealing that security checks confir...

item.title: Bien represents East Africa at Afro+ Fest 2026 in the US
item.description: Kenyan singer Bien, a former member of Sauti Sol, is set to represent East Africa at Afro+ Fest 2026, a major music event in the United States. The festival will feature a diverse lineup of global and...

item.title: Telstra Outage Caused by Aging Hardware: A Warning for Businesses
item.description: Telstra's massive national outage, affecting 45% of calls and data, stemmed from a failed software update on a 2011 server valued at $30,000. The hardware, responsible for maintaining network time acc...

item.title: Comelec evaluates Ombudsman's proposal to enhance COC transparency
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) is reviewing the Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla's suggestion to amend the rules for filing certificates of candidacy (COCs). Remulla proposed requiring candidate...

item.title: Trump Faces Criticism for Delayed Response to Soldier Deaths in Iranian Attacks
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump drew widespread criticism for his delayed and dismissive response to the deaths of three American soldiers killed in Iranian drone attacks over the weekend. The victims, Ja...

item.title: Jelly Roll and Bunnie Xo finalize divorce after decade-long marriage
item.description: Country singer Jelly Roll and his ex-wife, Bunnie Xo, have reportedly finalized their divorce after nearly a decade of marriage. According to court documents obtained by multiple outlets, the couple e...

item.title: BARMM Police Ordered to Maintain Neutrality Ahead of Parliamentary Elections
item.description: The Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) police have been instructed to remain non-partisan as security preparations intensify for the region's historic parliamentary elections sche...

item.title: Vehicle Collides with Hydro Pole in Barrie, Ontario
item.description: A vehicle crashed into a hydro pole in Barrie, Ontario, on July 21, 2026. The incident occurred during evening hours, prompting local authorities to investigate the cause and assess any damage to infr...
